assistant.title: AI Assistant

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.message: The page {{path}} does not exist or has been moved.
notFound.humor: Even spiders sometimes get lost in their own web.

dashboard.tip1: This dashboard provides a synthetic view of your server traffic over the selected period.
dashboard.tip2: KPIs at the top summarise human visits, bots, errors and the overall error rate.
dashboard.tip3: Hover over ⓘ icons for detailed explanations of each indicator.
dashboard.tip4: Use the date picker at the top right to change the analysed period.
dashboard.totalRequestsInfo: Total number of HTTP requests received by your server over the period.
dashboard.humanVisitorsInfo: Requests from human browsers (excluding detected bots).
dashboard.errorRateInfo: Percentage of requests that returned a 4xx or 5xx code.
dashboard.unique404Info: Number of distinct URLs that returned a 404 over the period.
dashboard.httpEvolutionInfo: This chart shows the evolution of request counts by HTTP code family over the selected period.
dashboard.httpEvolutionImpact: A rise in 4xx indicates missing pages (404) or access issues. 5xx signals server errors.
dashboard.httpEvolutionAction: Analyse error spikes and identify affected URLs in 'Top Pages'.
dashboard.botsDistributionInfo: Distribution of crawl bots that visited your site.
dashboard.botsDistributionImpact: Googlebot should represent a significant share. A low count indicates a crawlability issue.
dashboard.trendsContent: Evolution of traffic, Googlebot and TTFB over the past 12 weeks.
dashboard.trendsImpact: Identify long-term trends: drop in Googlebot crawl, progressive TTFB degradation, or bot surge.
dashboard.trendsAction: If average TTFB exceeds 800ms over several weeks, investigate server performance.

httpCodes.tip1: 2xx codes indicate successfully loaded pages. 3xx are redirects.
httpCodes.tip2: 404s signal missing pages — fix or redirect them to preserve SEO.
httpCodes.tip3: 5xx reveal server errors — critical if frequent, as they block Google crawl.
httpCodes.tip4: Filter by code and export to CSV or Excel to work with other tools.
httpCodes.kpi3xxInfo: Too many redirects waste crawl budget. Favour direct redirects.
httpCodes.kpi4xxInfo: Missing pages or access denied. 404s crawled by Googlebot waste crawl budget.
httpCodes.kpi5xxInfo: Critical server-side errors. Fix as a priority.
httpCodes.chartContent: Each point represents the request volume of an HTTP code family for a given day.
httpCodes.chartImpact: 4xx/5xx spikes often correlate with problematic deployments or attacks.

topPages.tip1: The "404 Errors" tab lists the most requested missing URLs — opportunities to create 301 redirects.
topPages.tip2: A 404 heavily visited by Googlebot wastes your crawl budget and harms indexation.
topPages.tip3: The "Top Pages" tab shows your most popular content — useful for prioritising optimisation.
topPages.tip4: Click a URL to copy it or open it directly in your browser.
topPages.info404Content: Missing pages: the requested resource no longer exists or the URL is incorrect.
topPages.info404Impact: 404s frequently crawled by Googlebot waste your crawl budget and may cause de-indexation.
topPages.info404Action: Create 301 redirects to the most relevant page, or return 410 Gone if the page should no longer exist.

bots.tip1: Bots are automated robots that visit your site — some are useful (Googlebot), others are not.
bots.tip2: Googlebot is Google's indexing robot: if it stops appearing, your site may no longer be indexed.
bots.tip3: A high bot/human ratio may indicate scraping or attacks — watch for unusual spikes.
bots.tip4: "Crawl budget" is Googlebot's capacity to explore your site: optimise it by fixing 404 and 5xx errors.
bots.kpiBotRequestsInfo: Total requests from detected crawl bots.
bots.kpiGooglebotInfo: Googlebot visit count. A low number may indicate a crawlability issue.
bots.kpiBotRatioInfo: Percentage of traffic consumed by bots. A high ratio may indicate aggressive crawling or attacks.
bots.chartDistributionContent: Distribution of different robots crawling your site. Ideally Googlebot should dominate among SEO bots.
bots.chartDistributionImpact: A high ratio of third-party SEO bots (Ahrefs, Semrush) vs Googlebot may indicate your site is more monitored by competitors than crawled by Google.

ttfb.tip1: TTFB (Time To First Byte) measures the time between the request and the first byte returned by the server.
ttfb.tip2: Google considers TTFB ≤ 200ms as excellent, 200ms–800ms as acceptable, above as slow.
ttfb.tip3: A high TTFB may be caused by a slow database, lack of cache, or overloaded server.
ttfb.tip4: Enable the "Slow pages" filter to quickly identify priority URLs to optimise.
ttfb.tip5: Adjust the "slow" threshold (in ms) to fit your performance goals.
ttfb.kpiAvgTTFBInfo: Average time between the HTTP request and the first byte received by the client. Target: < 200ms (excellent) or < 800ms (acceptable).
ttfb.kpiSlowPagesInfo: Number of requests that exceeded the {{threshold}}ms threshold.
ttfb.kpiSlowPercentInfo: Share of requests exceeding the configured threshold.
ttfb.kpiFastPagesInfo: Requests with excellent TTFB (≤ 200ms). Goal: maximise this number.
ttfb.chartEvolutionContent: Evolution of your server's average response time. A spike may indicate server overload, a bad deployment, or an attack.
ttfb.chartEvolutionImpact: Google measures TTFB to assess server performance. TTFB > 800ms may impact crawl and ranking.
ttfb.chartEvolutionAction: On a spike: check server logs, CPU/RAM resources, and slow database queries.

network.tip1: This view lists all IP addresses and browsers/bots that accessed your site.
network.tip2: Click an IP to see the URLs it visited and detect scraping or attacks.
network.tip3: An unknown bot (without a recognised name) with high volume may indicate a malicious scraper.
network.tip4: HTTP code distribution per IP helps identify IPs generating errors.
network.blockModalInfo: The IP will be saved to your blocklist. Spider-Lens does not block traffic directly — export the rules from the Blocklist / Export page and apply them on your server.

anomalies.tip1: Spider-Lens analyses your traffic every hour and compares volumes to the last 7 days.
anomalies.tip2: An anomaly is triggered when a value exceeds the mean + 2.5 times the standard deviation.
anomalies.tip3: Critical anomalies trigger an email — warnings are recorded silently.
anomalies.tip4: At least 3 days of historical data are needed for reliable detection.
anomalies.emptyTextNew: Everything looks normal — detection improves with more historical data.

blocklist.tip1: This list contains IPs you have marked as blocked from the Network page.
blocklist.tip2: Export nginx or Apache rules to apply them on your web server.
blocklist.tip3: Spider-Lens does not block IPs directly — it generates rules to copy into your server config.
blocklist.tip4: To block a new IP, go to Network and click the 🚫 icon next to the IP.
blocklist.step1Body: From the Network view, click the + icon next to a suspicious IP to add it to this list.
blocklist.step2Body: Download the automatically generated nginx or Apache configuration file.
blocklist.step3Body: Copy the file to your server and include it in your config. Spider-Lens never modifies your server directly.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Spider-Lens configuration
settings.sectionSitesDesc: Each site corresponds to an Apache or Nginx log file
settings.weeklyDesc: Sent automatically every Monday at 8am by email and/or webhook
settings.weeklyContent: Weekly summary: traffic, top pages, 404s, bots, anomalies, TTFB
settings.reportHelpText: The report uses the SMTP config and webhook defined below. Enable at least one before activating the report.
settings.webhookDesc: Compatible with Discord, Slack, and any JSON HTTP POST endpoint
settings.webhookEnableDesc: Sends a notification for each detected anomaly
settings.webhookWarningsDesc: By default only critical anomalies are sent
settings.discordGuide: How to create a Discord webhook?
settings.discordStep1: Open the settings of the target Discord channel
settings.discordStep3: Copy the URL and paste it above
settings.confirmDeleteSite: Delete this site? Associated log data will be kept.

dbAdmin.unlimitedWarning: ⚠️ Without a retention limit, the database can grow indefinitely and degrade performance. We recommend at least 90 days.
dbAdmin.purgeDescription: Immediately applies the retention policy and compresses the DB file (VACUUM).

assistant.tip1: Ask natural language questions about your traffic: 'Why is Googlebot not crawling /contact?'
assistant.tip2: The assistant analyzes the last 30 days of data and gives you concrete recommendations.
assistant.tip3: Start with the automatic analysis to get an SEO health score and key insights.
assistant.chatPlaceholder: Ask a question about your logs...
assistant.welcome: Hello! I'm your SEO assistant. Run the automatic analysis or ask me a question directly.
assistant.noApiKey: The Gemini API key is not configured. Add GEMINI_API_KEY to your .env file.
assistant.errorGeneric: An error occurred. Please try again.
